text: The surgeon confirms the location of the splenic flexure tumor. Traction is applied to the inferior mesenteric vein, creating a plane between the mesocolon and the retroperitoneum. The inferior mesenteric vein is divided using ligature, and the plane is further developed.
The surgeon develops a plane between the pancreas and the mesocolon of the splenic flexure, mobilizing the tail of the pancreas from the mesocolon.

Traction is applied to the left branch of the middle colic artery, which is then skeletalized at its origin. The vessel is clipped and divided using a ligasure device. The surgeon divides the remaining mesentery, ensuring all associated lymphatic tissue with the left branch of the middle colic artery is excised en bloc.

The surgeon focuses on the descending colon mesentery, mobilizing it in a medial to lateral direction along the axis of the inferior mesenteric vein. Tension is applied to the inferior mesenteric artery pedicle, and a plane is developed between the inferior mesenteric artery and the retroperitoneum.

The surgeon continues mobilization of the colon in a medial to lateral fashion, applying tension to the trifurcation of the inferior mesenteric artery, superior hemorrhoidal artery, and left colic artery. The left colic artery and vein are carefully skeletalized at their origin.
The surgeon clips and divides the vessels individually using ligature, completing the medial mobilization of the colon.
The surgeon focuses on lateral mobilization, with the ureter clearly visible in the surgical field.
The surgeon continues lateral mobilization towards the spleen, separating the omentum from the transverse colon.
The surgeon completes the immobilization of the splenic flexure by carefully dissecting the mesentery and associated omentum from the tail of the pancreas, revealing the medial plane of dissection.

The fully exposed tail of the pancreas is visible. The surgical specimen is extracted through the umbilicus, resected, and an end-to-end anastomosis is performed manually. Perfusion of the anastomosis is confirmed.
